generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Prioritize repairs that address unresolved identifiers or type mismatches reported by the AGREE validator.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helper variables within AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Ensure correct scalar type declarations (int, real, bool) in AGREE contracts.
Use `eq` helpers to define reusable expressions within AGREE contracts.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to repair AGREE syntax by modifying the AADL architecture.
Do not use subcomponent paths directly in component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction details (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, owners)
